title: 13 Branch Capture Reports and Research
body_text:
13	Branch Capture Reports and Research
13.1	Overview of Branch Capture Reports and Research
This chapter provides procedures for generating deposit reports and using the deposit research function in Branch Capture.
Note:	Deposit information is available in Branch Capture up to180 days after processing. Alternatively, Central 1 Clients can perform item research in the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures. ORS provides a seven-year history of deposits and deposit items.
Capture, Reviewer, and Supervisor Users generate reports from a deposit or from the list of deposits in Branch Capture. See Section 13.2, Generating a Branch Capture Deposit Item Details Report, and Section 13.3, Generating a Branch Capture Deposit List Report, for more information.
Supervisor and Administrator Users can generate custom deposit reports and use the Branch Capture research function. See the related sections in this chapter for procedures.
13.2	Generating a Branch Capture Deposit Item Details Report
A Deposit Items Details report contains summary and detailed information for a deposit. The reportГўВҖВҷs summary and detail information reflect the depositГўВҖВҷs state at the time the report is generated. For example, if you generate a report before the deposit is transmitted, it wonГўВҖВҷt include the transmitted date/time. Capture Users can generate a Deposit Items Detail report for deposits that they create or are assigned. Reviewer and Supervisor Users can generate a report for all deposits at assigned locations.
Generate a Branch Capture Deposit Item Details report as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Search for and open the appropriate deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Select
Report View
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Select ГўВҖВңReport ViewГўВҖВқ
View the report. Select
Print Report
to print the report. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: View and Print Report
13.3	Generating a Branch Capture Deposit List Report
A Branch Capture Deposit List report provides a list of deposits from the Deposit List page. Capture Users can generate a deposit list report for all the deposits that they create or are assigned. Reviewer or Supervisor Users can generate a report for all deposits at assigned locations.
Generate a Branch Capture Deposit List report as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Go to the Deposit List as described in Section 9.6, Section Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Update the List of Deposits filter if required. The default is the current date.
Select
Report View
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Generating a List of Deposits Report
View the report. Select
Print
to print the report. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Print Report
13.4	Generating a Custom Deposit Details Report in Branch Capture
Supervisor and Administrator Users can generate a custom Deposit Details Report for specific timeframes, locations, and/or deposit states.
Generate a custom Deposit Details report as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Select
Reports
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Select ГўВҖВңReportsГўВҖВқ
Complete the report options as follows. See Figure 6.
ГўВ‘
Select
Deposit Details
from the Report drop-down menu.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the appropriate report output type (
DOCX
,
PDF
, or
RTF
).
ГўВ‘Вў
Select the appropriate Date Range Type:
ГўВҲВ’
Create Date
ГўВҖВ“ Use if the report must contain deposits in an Open state. See Section 9.14, Reviewing a Branch Capture Deposit for deposit state descriptions.
ГўВҲВ’
Deposit Receipt Date
ГўВҖВ“ Use if the report must contain only transmitted deposits.
Note:	The Posting Date filter isnГўВҖВҷt applicable and is considered the same as the Receipt Date.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Select a valid date range from the Start and End Date menu.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Select a location from the Location drop-down menu. If you select
All
, the report includes all locations and all associated accounts in the same report. The report is sorted by location.
Note:	If you select a specific location, a Bank Account drop-down menu displays. You can select a specific account (e.g., 03-080980430-001 ГўВҖВ“ CAD-DB), or you can select
All
to include all accounts in the report. Leave the User ID field as ГўВҖВңAllГўВҖВқ.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Select one of the following states from the Deposit Status drop-down menu:
ГўВҲВ’
Select
All
to include all deposit states.
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Open
to include deposits that havenГўВҖВҷt been transmitted.
Note:	To include Open deposits, set the Date Range Type option to ГўВҖВңCreate DateГўВҖВқ or you will get an empty report.
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Received
for deposits that are transmitted.
Note:	ГўВҖВңTransmittingГўВҖВқ, ГўВҖВңReceived FailedГўВҖВқ, ГўВҖВңPerfectedГўВҖВқ, or ГўВҖВңPerfected AdjustedГўВҖВқ are transitory states and donГўВҖВҷt apply.
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Ensure that you select
No Images
in the Image drop-down menu. Images shouldnГўВҖВҷt be included in reports. If you require images, Central 1 Clients can perform item research on the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures.
ГўВ‘В§
Leave Capture Source as ГўВҖВңAll SourcesГўВҖВқ.
Note: 	ГўВҖВңMobileГўВҖВқ displays in the drop-down menu, but mobile deposit information isnГўВҖВҷt available in Branch Capture.
Select
Create Report
.
Figure 6: Completing the Deposit Detail Report Options
The report request displays in the Report Instances section. If a report successfully generates, the State column for the report request displays as ГўВҖВңCompletedГўВҖВқ. See Figure 7. Choose from the following tasks:
ГўВ‘
Select the View icon to download the report.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the Item History icon to view the report parameters.
ГўВ‘Вў
Select the Delete icon to delete the report.
Figure 7: Report Tasks
Note:	If thereГўВҖВҷs no data matching the search parameters, the State column for the report request displays as ГўВҖВңEmptyГўВҖВқ and the ГўВҖВңViewГўВҖВқ and ГўВҖВңDeleteГўВҖВқ tasks donГўВҖВҷt display.
13.5	Generating a Custom Deposit Details by Deposit Number Report in Branch Capture
Supervisor and Administrator Users can generate a custom Deposit Details by Deposit Number report for a specific range of deposit numbers.
Generate a Custom Deposit Details by Deposit Number report as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Select
Reports
. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Select ГўВҖВңReportsГўВҖВқ
Complete the report options as follows. See Figure 9.
ГўВ‘
Select
Deposit Details by Deposit Number
from the Report drop-down menu.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the appropriate report type (
DOCX
,
PDF
, or
RTF
).
ГўВ‘Вў
Enter a range of deposit numbers in the Deposit Number fields.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Ensure that you select
No Images
in the Image drop-down menu. Images shouldnГўВҖВҷt be included in reports. If you require images, Central 1 Clients can perform item research on the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Leave Capture Source as ГўВҖВңAll SourcesГўВҖВқ.
Note: 	ГўВҖВңMobileГўВҖВқ displays in the drop-down menu, however mobile deposit information isnГўВҖВҷt available in Branch Capture.
Select
Create Report
.
Figure 9: Completing the Deposit Details by Deposit Number Report Options
The report request displays in the Report Instances section. If a report successfully generates, the State column for the report request displays as ГўВҖВңCompletedГўВҖВқ. See Figure 10. Choose from the following tasks:
ГўВ‘
Select the View icon to download the report.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the Item History icon to view the report parameters.
ГўВ‘Вў
Select the Delete icon to delete the report.
Figure 10: Report Tasks
Note:	If thereГўВҖВҷs no data matching the search parameters, the State column for the report request displays as ГўВҖВңEmptyГўВҖВқ and the ГўВҖВңViewГўВҖВқ and ГўВҖВңDeleteГўВҖВқ icons donГўВҖВҷt display.
13.6	Generating a Custom Deposit Summary Report in Branch Capture
Supervisor and Administrator Users can generate a Deposit Summary report for specific timeframes, locations, and/or deposit states.
To generate a Custom Deposit Summary Report:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Select
Reports
. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Select ГўВҖВңReportsГўВҖВқ
Complete the report options as follows. See Figure 12.
ГўВ‘
Select
Deposit Summary
from the Report drop-down menu.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the appropriate report output type (DOCX, PDF, or RTF).
ГўВ‘Вў
Select the Date Range Type:
ГўВҲВ’
Create Date
ГўВҖВ“ Use if the report must contain deposits in an Open state.
ГўВҲВ’
Deposit Receipt Date
ГўВҖВ“ Use if the report must contain only transmitted deposits.
Note:	The Posting Date filter isnГўВҖВҷt applicable and is considered the same as the Receipt Date.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Select a valid date range from the Start and End Date menu.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Select a location from the Location drop-down menu. If you select
All
, the report includes all locations and all associated accounts in the same report. The report is sorted by location.
Note:	If you select a specific location, a ГўВҖВңBank AccountГўВҖВқ drop-down menu displays. You can select a specific account (e.g., 03-080980430-001 ГўВҖВ“ CAD-DB), or you can select
All
to include all accounts in the report. Leave the User ID field at ГўВҖВңAllГўВҖВқ.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Select one of the following states from the Deposit Status drop-down menu:
ГўВҲВ’
Select
All
to include all deposit states.
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Open
to include deposits that have not been transmitted.
Note:	To include Open deposits, set the Date Range Type option to ГўВҖВңCreate DateГўВҖВқ or you will get an empty report.
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Received
for deposits that are transmitted.
Note:	ГўВҖВңTransmittingГўВҖВқ, ГўВҖВңReceived FailedГўВҖВқ, ГўВҖВңPerfectedГўВҖВқ, or ГўВҖВңPerfected AdjustedГўВҖВқ donГўВҖВҷt apply.
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Ensure that you select
No Images
in the Image drop-down menu. Images shouldnГўВҖВҷt be included in reports. If you require images, Central 1 Clients can perform item research on the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures.
Select
Create Report
.
Figure 12: Completing the Deposit Summary Report Options
The report request displays in the Report Instances section. If a report successfully generates, the State column for the report request displays as ГўВҖВңCompletedГўВҖВқ. See Figure 13. Choose from the following tasks:
ГўВ‘
Select the View icon to download the report.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the Item History icon to view the report parameters.
ГўВ‘Вў
Select the Delete icon to delete the report.
Figure 13: Report Tasks
Note:	If thereГўВҖВҷs no data matching the search parameters, the State column for the report request displays as ГўВҖВңEmptyГўВҖВқ and the ГўВҖВңViewГўВҖВқ and ГўВҖВңDeleteГўВҖВқ icons donГўВҖВҷt display.
13.7	Using the Branch Capture Research Function
The Branch Capture research function allows Supervisors and Administrators to search for branch capture deposit items and generate customized item research reports. Search results that users generate using the Branch Capture research function are only available during the userГўВҖВҷs current session. Once the user logs out of Branch Capture, the search results are deleted.
Users can download or print a report of the research results from either the Item Research ГўВҖВ“ Query page or from the Item Research ГўВҖВ“ Stored Results page. See the related subsection(s) in this section.
Note:	Alternatively, Central 1 Clients can perform item research on the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures. ORS provides a 7-year history of deposits and deposit items.
Researching Branch Capture Items
Research Branch Capture Items as follows:
ГўВҖВў	Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
ГўВҖВў	Select
Research
. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Select ГўВҖВңResearchГўВҖВқ
Enter search criteria, then select
Search
. See Figure 16. You can select up to five different search elements. Depending on the criteria that you select, you may need to complete additional fields in the Values column (e.g., if you select ГўВҖВңDeposit DateГўВҖВқ, you must enter a date range).
Note:	Select
Clear
to remove the selected search criteria and start over.
Figure 16: Enter Search Criteria
View the search results. A maximum of 1000 deposit items display. Use the page navigation to view additional results. See Figure 17.
Note:	Modify the search criteria to reduce the number of results, if necessary. Select
Clear List
to remove the search results.
Figure 17: Research Results
Preview an item from your research results as follows:
Select the Preview icon. The front image of the item displays on the query results page. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Select the Preview Icon
View the item and the related deposit as follows:
Select the View icon on the appropriate item. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: Select the View Icon
View the item and related deposit. Select
Return to Research
to go back to the query page. See Figure 20.
Figure 20: Viewing the Deposit Item and Deposit
Generating Research Reports
You can create a report from search results or add multiple queries together to create a report using the Stored Results option.
Generating a Research Report from Search Results
Create a report from search results as follows. See Figure 21.
ГўВ‘
Select the check box next to each item you want to include in the report. Alternatively, choose
Select All
.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the appropriate report output type (
CSV
,
PDF
,
RTF
, or
XLS
).
ГўВ‘Вў
Ensure that you select
No Images
in the Images drop-down menu. If you require images, CentralВ 1 Clients can perform item research on the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Select
Create Report
. Depending on your browser type, you may need to save the report, or it may download automatically. See Figure 22 for a sample PDF report.
Figure 21: Creating a Report from Search Results
Figure 22: Sample PDF Report
Generating a Research Report from Stored Results
Use Stored Results to add multiple queries together to create a report as follows:
Enter search criteria as previously described.
Select the check box next to each item that you want to include in the report, then select
Add to Stored Results
. See Figure 23.
Figure 23: Add Items to Stored Results
Repeat the previous steps until youГўВҖВҷve selected all items to include in the report, then select
Go to Stored Results
. See Figure 24.
Figure 24: Select ГўВҖВңGo to Stored ResultsГўВҖВқ
Generate the report from stored results as follows. See Figure 25.
ГўВ‘
Select the check box next to each item that you want to include in the report. Alternatively, choose
Select All
.
Note:	To remove an item from stored results, select the check box next to the appropriate item, the select
Remove from Results
.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the appropriate report output type (
CSV
,
PDF
,
RTF
, or
XLS
).
ГўВ‘Вў
Ensure you select
No Images
in the Images drop-down menu. If you require images, perform item research on the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Select
Create Report
. Depending on your browser, you may need to save the report, or it may download automatically. See Figure 25 for a sample PDF report.
Note:	Select
Return to Query
to go back to your search.
Figure 24: Generating a Report from Stored Results
Figure 25: Sample PDF Report
END
